I did some etymology looking into why they were called Blow Game recently, found out it's because it's meant to be like "one hit, the fatal blow" and 打 means hit in that context, but also blow, so Conquer The Blow Game = Beat The One Hit Game

boshy moment
hoes does bad colission order even happenm
how does*
like most spikes you can't wall hug on in boshy
gamemaker detects what you're colliding with in order of the resource tree
so if you make spikes before making blocks, the spikes will kill you first
huh
thats how it works in studio
i thought that was how it worked in gm8 as well?
so basically that wont happen if u use a normal engine then?
basically gamemaker lets you make collision events instead of detecting collision via place_meeting or whatever, and the order those events are done is determined differently by gamemaker version
studio does it by object order in the resource tree, while gm8 does it by which ones were made first
the issue is engine independent, it only happens if you move blocks below spikes in the engine
so if you have a custom block and a custom spike, if you make the custom spike first you will have issues
you can prevent all of this by not using collision events and just checking for it manually
